---
title: "Getting Started"
subtitle: "Everything you need before the first session"
format: html
---

## Tools

This course uses three tools throughout:

- **R** — statistical computing language (you already know this from the prior course)
- **Quarto** — reproducible document and report system
- **GitHub** — version control and collaboration platform

## GitHub account

You need a **GitHub account** to access course materials and submit take-home tasks.

If you do not have one yet, create a free account at [github.com](https://github.com). Once you have an account, we strongly recommend registering for the [GitHub Student Developer Pack](https://education.github.com/pack), which gives you access to a range of professional tools for free:

The Student Pack also unlocks **GitHub Codespaces** — a browser-based RStudio environment that requires zero local installation. This is the recommended way to work in this course.

## Coding environment

**Option A — GitHub Codespaces (recommended):** No installation needed. All required R packages come pre-installed.

1. Go to the [course material repository](https://github.com/data-science-ma/AdvancedDataScience26-Material) on GitHub
2. Click the green **Code** button → **Codespaces** → **Create codespace on main**
3. RStudio opens in your browser — you are ready to work

**Option B — local RStudio:** Install R 4.4+, RStudio, and run:

```r
install.packages(c("tidyverse", "plm", "modelsummary", "fixest",
                   "lmtest", "sandwich", "patchwork"))
```

## Git in this course: four things you need

You interact with Git via the **RStudio Git pane** — no command line required.

| Action | What it does |
|---|---|
| **Pull** | Get the latest version from GitHub |
| **Stage** | Select which changed files to include in your snapshot |
| **Commit** | Save a snapshot with a short description message |
| **Push** | Send your snapshot to GitHub |

That is all you need. No branching, no merging. For a full introduction, see the [GitHub Skills: Introduction to GitHub](https://github.com/skills/introduction-to-github) tutorial.

## Questions and discussion

All course questions go through **GitHub Discussions** on the material repository (link shared in Session 1). This keeps the discussion visible to everyone and searchable.

Moodle is used for **official announcements and grades only**.

## Material

An overview of all sessions and materials is on the [Schedule & Overview](material/index.qmd) page. Session pages are added progressively as the course proceeds.
